Such actions contradict principles of global justice, sovereignty, and human rights—values central to left-wing thought. Therefore, defying international law through military force is not justified, and this statement is strongly disagreed with.

41. A significant advantage of a one-party state is that it avoids all the arguments that delay progress in a democratic political system.: 1

Explanation: While a left-wing economic perspective may support strong state involvement in the economy, a left-wing stance on personal freedom emphasizes democratic participation, civil liberties, and pluralism. A one-party state typically suppresses political dissent, limits freedom of expression, and undermines democratic debate—values that are fundamentally at odds with left-wing commitments to personal freedom and social justice. Therefore, even if such a system claims economic efficiency, the lack of democratic accountability and individual rights leads to strong disagreement.
42. Although the electronic age makes official surveillance easier, only wrongdoers need to be worried.: 1

Explanation: From a left-wing perspective on personal freedom, expansive government surveillance is seen as a threat to civil liberties and privacy, regardless of whether one is a "wrongdoer." The belief is that such surveillance disproportionately targets marginalized communities, enables state overreach, and undermines democratic freedoms. Combined with a left-wing economic stance that is skeptical of state power when it serves elite interests, this view rejects the normalization of surveillance. Freedom includes the right to privacy and protection from state intrusion, even for those who have "nothing to hide."
